reasoning: Between 2004 and 2024, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced about 9 component change events (rough average of ~0.43 adjustment events per year). The time remaining from the April 25, 2025 knowledge cutoff until the July 1, 2025 resolution date is a little over two months (~18% of a year). The naive base rate gives a ~7.5% chance of an event in any random 2-month period. However, considering S&P Dow Jones Indices made two separate changes in 2024 (Amazon replacing Walgreens in February; Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams replacing Intel and Dow Inc. in November) to maintain sector balance and price relevance, another imminent change in mid-2025 is unlikely. The index committee makes adjustments "on an as-needed basis" and typically pauses after recent rebalancing unless forced by a major corporate event such as an M&A, extreme price crash, or massive stock split causing divisor distortions. Broad web searches across Q1/April 2025 reveal no credible rumors or announcements of an impending removal from the Dow 30 components. Although some opinion pieces occasionally suggest dropping underperformers like Boeing, these lack substantive committee backing. Therefore, the probability scales down from the baseline to about 4%.
